heading: Subsistence for prisoners
label: 4006 Subsistence for prisoners
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 703 (R.S. § 5545; , ).Mar. 2, 1911, ch. 19236 Stat. 1003The provisions relating to the Washington Asylum and Jail are now included in the District of Columbia Code. (See D.C. Code, 1940 ed., § 24–421.)Changes of phraseology were made.

References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (b)(1), is , . Title XVIII of the Act is classified generally to subchapter XVIII (§ 1395 et seq.) of chapter 7 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Attorney General or the Secretary of Homeland Security, as applicable, shall allow and pay only the reasonable and actual cost of the subsistence of prisoners in the custody of any marshal of the United States, and shall prescribe such regulations for the government of the marshals as will enable him to determine the actual and reasonable expenses incurred.

children.heading: Health Care Items and Services.—
children.label: b Health Care Items and Services.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Payment for costs incurred for the provision of health care items and services for individuals in the custody of the United States Marshals Service, the Federal Bureau of Investigation and the Department of Homeland Security shall be the amount billed, not to exceed the amount that would be paid for the provision of similar health care items and services under the Medicare program under title XVIII of the Social Security Act.

children.heading: .—Full and final payment
children.label: 2 .—Full and final payment
children.text: Any payment for a health care item or service made pursuant to this subsection, shall be deemed to be full and final payment.
